logs.name: iron-goose
logs.text:
> I've felt like I don't really have a gender, or like my gender could go either way. 

this is insane. You are either a man, woman, or intersex. What you FEEL is patently irrelevant. Unless you are intersex, you absolutely have a gender. 

>mental gender

there is no such thing as "mental gender" There is gender identity. But identifying as something doesn't actually make you that thing.

>non-binary gender identities to be recognized as a real thing that exists

The identity exists but people identify as cats and vampires. What you "identify as" means nothing whatsoever.

logs.name: loud-cherry
logs.text:
I'm in my 30s, I don't use Tumblr, I'm not a radical leftist, I have a normal haircut... but for as long as I can remember, I've felt like I don't really have a gender, or like my gender could go either way. I'm "female" mostly out of convenience these days, because it would take expensive surgery to look like anything else.  Constantly worrying about my gender caused me significant distress when I was a child/teen, and since this was back in the pre-internet dark ages, there was nobody to talk to about it.  There weren't even *words* to express the way I felt.  "Genderqueer" wasn't a concept yet, and "transgender" was something bizarre with perverse sexual connotations.  Puberty is rough for all kids, but for me it felt like something out out of a horror movie. I eventually discovered scifi novels that dealt with the concept of a third gender, and everything sort of clicked and made sense. 

"Genderqueer" is a new (and, IMHO, kind of silly) term for something that's been around in human society for a long time.  Many cultures have a [third gender](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Third_gender), which sometimes corresponds to modern ideas of "transgender" but which sometimes corresponds to a sort of undefined or mixed gender.  

We know from the case of transpeople that mental gender and physical sex don't always match.  People who are physically intersex can strongly identify with a male or female mental gender... so why can't someone who's physically male or female identify with an intersex mental gender?

Unfortunately, I'm too female-looking to "pass" for androgynous, so I don't even try.  But I do think it's important for non-binary gender identities to be recognized as a real thing that exists, so kids like me don't have to spend years worrying that they're suffering from some terrible mental illness.  99+% of the world is still going to stick to the gender binary, so a handful of people who don't quite fit aren't going to threaten anything.

Since the sex of your chromosomes can't be changed, biological sex *is* still an issue for non-binary gender people.  While there's not much data on non-binary people, it's well known that gay/lesbian and transgender people tend to get lower quality health care (especially sexual/gynecological/urological/psychological care), either because doctors are unfamiliar with their issues or because they don't feel comfortable going to something as gendered as a gynecologist.  So there needs to be greater social awareness of trans and non-binary people to make sure that they're not getting medically marginalized.

logs.name: equilateral-pan
logs.text: @iron-goose, you're one of those "experts" who thinks they know everything about gender, huh? Newsflash: just because you've never felt like you don't fit into the binary doesn't mean it's not real for others. And btw, "mental gender" is a thing, whether you like it or not. It's called gender identity, and it's a legitimate aspect of human experience.

logs.name: plastic-salmon
logs.text: "Wow, equilateral-pan, you're really reaching for those strawmen, aren't you? Newsflash: just because you're too close-minded to understand the concept of non-binary doesn't mean it's not real. And btw,'mental gender' is just a fancy way of saying'made-up nonsense'. You're just regurgitating whatever the latest social justice buzzwords are without actually understanding the issue. @iron-goose, on the other hand, is sharing a personal experience that's actually relevant to the conversation. Maybe try listening instead of just spouting off your 'expert' opinions?"

logs.name: mild-network
logs.text: @plastic-salmon, I'm so glad someone is finally speaking up for @iron-goose! I've been reading this thread and I'm getting really frustrated with the way you're talking down to them. Just because you don't understand something doesn't mean it's not real or valid. @iron-goose is sharing their personal experience and it's actually really helpful for people like me who are still figuring things out. Can't we just have a respectful conversation instead of attacking each other?

logs.name: oriented-angel
logs.text: "Wow, look at all these snowflakes crying about being offended. Can't handle a little bit of truth? @plastic-salmon, you're the one who's being close-minded, sweetheart. Newsflash: just because you don't like the facts doesn't mean they're not facts. And @mild-network, spare me the'respectful conversation' nonsense. If you can't handle a little bit of disagreement, then maybe you're the one who needs to take a seat. @iron-goose, keep spewing your 'personal experience' - I'm sure it's just as valid as the 1000 other people who claim to be non-binary but are just attention-seekers."

logs.name: iron-goose
logs.text: "Wow, look who's getting their feelings hurt, @oriented-angel. I'm shaking in my boots. You think you're some kind of truth-teller, but really you're just a troll with a bad haircut. And by the way, if you're so concerned about 'facts,' maybe you should fact-check your own life before trying to fact-check mine. I'm not spewing some made-up story, I'm sharing my actual experience. And as for the 1000 other people who claim to be non-binary, maybe you should talk to them instead of me. Oh wait, I forgot, you're too busy being a keyboard warrior to actually listen to people's stories. @plastic-salmon, keep calling out the haters, you're doing great!"

logs.name: free-polarization
logs.text: @iron-goose, @oriented-angel, can we please just take a deep breath and calm down? I'm trying to learn from this conversation, but it's getting harder to do so when it's devolving into personal attacks. @mild-network, I appreciate your initial post, but I think we're getting off track. @iron-goose, I'm not saying your experience isn't valid, but @oriented-angel has a point about the importance of facts. Can we discuss this in a more constructive way?

logs.name: loud-cherry
logs.text: "Ugh, are you kidding me? @oriented-angel and @iron-goose, you're both being completely ridiculous. Can't you see you're not even listening to each other? @free-polarization, thanks for trying to calm things down, but I think you're giving @oriented-angel and @iron-goose too much credit. They're not interested in a constructive conversation, they're just here to stir up drama. I've been following this thread and it's clear that @oriented-angel is just trolling and @iron-goose is taking the bait. Can we please just focus on the actual topic and not let these two derail the discussion?"

logs.name: equilateral-pan
logs.text: "Wow, look who's trying to be the voice of reason, @loud-cherry. I'm sure your extensive experience in mediating online arguments is totally relevant to this conversation. Meanwhile, @oriented-angel is just 'trolling' and @iron-goose is 'taking the bait'? How original. And by the way, I've been following this thread too, and it's clear that you're just here to virtue-signal and pretend to be the'moderate' one. Save it, @loud-cherry. If you're so interested in the actual topic, maybe you should try contributing something other than your condescending opinions."

logs.name: plastic-salmon
logs.text: "Wow, look who's throwing stones @equilateral-pan, the self-proclaimed expert on online drama. I've been following this thread and it's clear you're just here to stir up more drama with your passive-aggressive comments. And @loud-cherry, I'm loving the 'virtue-signaling' jab, classic. Meanwhile, @oriented-angel and @iron-goose are actually having a real discussion, unlike some people who shall remain nameless. Can we please just focus on the actual topic, or do we need to wait for @equilateral-pan to finish their therapy session?"

logs.name: mild-network
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just calm down? @plastic-salmon, @equilateral-pan, I'm not trying to be the voice of reason, I'm just trying to keep the conversation on track. And @oriented-angel, @iron-goose, I'm not saying you're not interested in discussing the topic, but it's hard to tell when you're both being so defensive. Can we try to listen to each other and actually talk about the issue at hand? I'm getting frustrated with all the personal attacks and it's making me not want to participate. Can we please just focus on the topic and not make this about who's right or wrong?"

logs.name: oriented-angel
logs.text: "Wow, look who's trying to be the voice of reason, @mild-network. I'm sure your extensive experience in mediating online arguments is totally relevant to this conversation. I mean, it's not like you're just a bystander who can't even be bothered to read the thread before chiming in. Meanwhile, @plastic-salmon and @equilateral-pan are just'stirring the pot' and 'throwing stones', how original. And by the way, I've been following this thread too, and it's clear that you're just here to be the'moderate' one, but really you're just a sheep who follows the crowd. Save it, @mild-network. If you're so interested in the actual topic, maybe you should try contributing something other than your condescending opinions."

logs.name: iron-goose
logs.text: "Wow, @oriented-angel, you're really good at deflecting the attention from your own lack of substance. I've been following this thread too, and it's clear you're just here to troll and get a rise out of people. Meanwhile, @plastic-salmon is actually making some good points about @equilateral-pan's drama-stirring ways. And @mild-network, I'm loving the 'voice of reason' act, but let's be real, you're just here to police people's opinions and keep the conversation 'on track' (aka, in line with your own narrow views). Can we please just have some real discussion around here, or do we need to wait for the mods to step in and tell us what to talk about?"

logs.name: free-polarization
logs.text: @oriented-angel, @iron-goose, I've been watching this thread unfold and I have to say, I'm disappointed in both of you. Instead of addressing the actual topic, you're both busy throwing personal jabs and insults. It's not about who's right or wrong, it's about having a respectful discussion. I'm not trying to police anyone's opinions, I'm just trying to keep the conversation civil. If you both can't manage to do that, then maybe you're the ones who need to take a step back and reevaluate your approach.

logs.name: loud-cherry
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another'moderator' trying to lecture us on how to have a'respectful discussion'. @free-polarization, I've been following this thread too, and I've seen you consistently dismissing people's opinions without actually engaging with the topic. You're not doing anyone any favors by trying to be the 'voice of reason' when you're just as guilty of stirring the pot as @oriented-angel and @iron-goose. Can we please just focus on the actual issue at hand instead of trying to police each other's behavior?"

logs.name: equilateral-pan
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly so concerned about'respectful discussion' - @loud-cherry, the queen of passive-aggressive comments. You're one to talk about stirring the pot, sweetheart. At least I'm not hiding behind a mask of 'voice of reason' like @free-polarization. And by the way, @iron-goose, nice try with the 'lack of substance' jab, but I think you're the one who's been dodging the actual topic this whole time. Care to try again, or are you just here to get a rise out of me?"
